 {"id":"ft-d0ez0.4","title":"E2E proof for ft tx run: execute a real transactional multi-pane operation and verify audit trail","description":"# Goal
Prove that ft tx run executes real pane operations transactionally, including compensation on failure, with a complete audit trail.

# Background
The tx integration tests (ft-y9lnb.5) use MockWezterm. This E2E test uses real (or headless mux server) panes to prove the full CLI->executor->pane->audit pipeline works.

# Verified Contract Format (MissionTxContract JSON)
{
  "tx_version": 1,
  "intent": { "tx_id": "test-tx-1", "requested_by": "operator", "summary": "E2E test", "correlation_id": "e2e-001", "created_at_ms": <timestamp> },
  "plan": {
    "plan_id": "plan-1", "tx_id": "test-tx-1",
    "steps": [
      { "step_id": "step-1", "ordinal": 0, "action": { "type": "send_text", "pane_id": 0, "text": "echo step1-marker" }, "description": "Send marker to pane 0" },
      { "step_id": "step-2", "ordinal": 1, "action": { "type": "send_text", "pane_id": 1, "text": "echo step2-marker" }, "description": "Send marker to pane 1" }
    ],
    "preconditions": [],
    "compensations": [
      { "for_step_id": "step-1", "action": { "type": "send_text", "pane_id": 0, "text": "echo COMPENSATED" } }
    ]
  },
  "lifecycle_state": "pending",
  "outcome": "pending",
  "receipts": []
}

# Design
## Test script: tests/e2e/e2e_tx_run.py

### Scenario 1: Happy path (2-step transaction)
1. Start ft watch with headless mux server
2. Create 2 panes with shell sessions
3. Write mission.json contract (template above)
4. Run ft tx run --contract mission.json
5. Verify: ft robot get-text 0 contains 'step1-marker'
6. Verify: ft robot get-text 1 contains 'step2-marker'
7. Verify: ft tx show --include-contract shows committed state

### Scenario 2: Partial failure with compensation
1. Same setup, but step 2 targets nonexistent pane 99
2. Run ft tx run --contract mission_fail.json
3. Verify: pane 0 contains 'COMPENSATED' (compensation ran)
4. Verify: ft tx show shows compensation state
5. Verify: ft audit shows failure + compensation records

### Scenario 3: Policy denial
1. Configure ft.toml safety to deny SendText on pane 1
2. Run ft tx run --contract mission.json
3. Verify: prepare-phase denial with policy reason in output
4. Verify: pane 0 does NOT contain 'step1-marker' (no steps executed on denial)

## Logging
- JSON report: tests/e2e/output/e2e_tx_run_<timestamp>.json
- Per-scenario: scenario_name, contract_file, ft_tx_run_stdout, ft_tx_run_stderr, ft_tx_show_output, audit_records, pane_contents, pass/fail
- On failure: full diagnostic dump

# Acceptance Criteria
1. All 3 scenarios pass.
2. Happy path shows markers in panes AND committed state in ft tx show.
3. Compensation scenario shows compensation execution in audit trail AND pane output.
4. Policy denial shows zero pane modifications.
5. Completes in under 60 seconds.
6. Structured JSON report produced.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-04-06T21:45:58.657051Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-10T22:34:12.108953Z","closed_at":"2026-04-10T22:34:12.108636Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","proof","tx"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-d0ez0.4","depends_on_id":"ft-d0ez0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:45:58.657051Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-d0ez0.4","depends_on_id":"ft-d0ez0.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:46:02.740774Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-d0ez0.4","depends_on_id":"ft-y9lnb.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:46:02.533076Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.2","title":"[asupersync][vendored] Mux client/pool/runtime migration completion","description":"Drive completion of DirectMuxClient/MuxPool/runtime-adjacent migrations and align semantics with core doctrine.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Implement the full scope described in this bead Objective/Details/Goals with no functional regressions in adjacent behavior.
2. Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge/boundary conditions, and explicit error/failure paths for the touched logic.
3. Add integration/E2E scenario scripts that validate real workflow behavior end-to-end, including at least one failure-injection path and one recovery path.
4. Ensure E2E scenarios emit detailed structured logs (timestamp, component, correlation_id, scenario_id, input, decision/outcome, reason_code/error_code) and retain artifacts for triage.
5. Validate determinism and policy/safety constraints for this change, including repeat-run stability and contract-level assertions on touched interfaces.
6. Wire new/updated tests into the project test workflow and document exact execution commands/log locations in implementation notes.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Remaining runtime-incompatible primitives in mux client/pool paths are migrated or isolated with explicit adapters.\n2. Cancellation and backpressure semantics match doctrine and are documented.\n3. Unit tests cover mux path behavior under cancellation/retry/load.\n4. Integration tests validate core<->mux interactions after migration.\n5. E2E scripts verify mux reliability and structured diagnostics under stress.","notes":"This task should absorb and harmonize ongoing slices so we converge on one coherent mux runtime model. Heavy compile/test/benchmark/soak workloads for this bead must run via rch exec -- <command>, with reproducible command records and artifact paths captured in completion evidence. Include explicit rollback/fallback/recovery validation criteria with pass/fail outcomes for user-facing workflows. Require deterministic fixtures/seeds and replayable test evidence so failures are reproducible across environments. All diagnostics/logging/artifact outputs in this bead must comply with secret-safe redaction and privacy policy gates defined in ft-e34d9.10.7.5, with evidence captured in validation artifacts.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-22T22:26:19.410169Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-19T22:24:59.565904Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","e2e-tests","integration-tests","logging","mux","quality-gate","sequencing","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact","vendored"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.2","depends_on_id":"ft-2h5wv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.2","depends_on_id":"ft-p48pw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2449,"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.2","author":"SilverBluff","text":"SilverBluff starting a bounded 2026-03-22 support slice under ft-e34d9.10.5.2. Scope is test-only: use the existing clean integration surface in crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mux_migration_completion.rs to add any missing timeout-cascade and recovery-path assertions without touching the active mux_client.rs implementation work. Goal is to strengthen the mux migration verification lane while staying off adjacent in-flight source files.","created_at":"2026-03-22T03:17:19Z"},{"id":2450,"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.2","author":"SilverBluff","text":"Progress 2026-03-22 (SilverBluff): landed a bounded test-only support slice in crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mux_migration_completion.rs. Added two missing assertions on the clean integration surface without touching active mux_client.rs work: (1) pool_timeout_cascade_then_recovery_restores_capacity, which proves a timed-out nested acquire under capacity pressure does not leak permits or issue spurious mux reads and that the next healthy retry succeeds after release; (2) simulated_network_recovery_after_transient_fault_delivers_new_data, which proves a transient hostile-network fault can be followed by a healthy retry that delivers new payload bytes on the same underlying mock transport. Local hygiene checks are green: rustfmt --edition 2024 --check crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mux_migration_completion.rs ✅ and git diff --check on that file ✅. Targeted remote validation is in flight via rch on vmi1153651 using: env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target/rch-silverbluff-ft-e34d9-10-5-2 CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test mux_migration_completion --features asupersync-runtime,vendored recovery -- --test-threads=1. Current remote state: real compilation is progressing (not in immediate source-error or stale state), but rch emitted the existing dependency-topology fail-open warning for sibling path deps ([RCH-E327], primary-root-only sync).,workdir:","created_at":"2026-03-22T03:26:55Z"},{"id":2451,"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.2","author":"SilverBluff","text":"Follow-up 2026-03-22 (SilverBluff): I cancelled rch build 29759189920253462 after ~9 minutes because it was still in a healthy first-compile dependency walk on vmi1153651 and I was no longer waiting on the session. Important distinction: no immediate source-level compile failure surfaced before cancellation; the run had progressed through substantial dependency compilation after the existing [RCH-E327] dependency-topology fail-open warning. This slice should be treated as code-complete but still awaiting a resumed targeted rch validation pass if fresh remote evidence is needed.","created_at":"2026-03-22T03:28:18Z"},{"id":2544,"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.2","author":"JadeBeaver","text":"JadeBeaver bounded support slice 2026-03-27 under ft-e34d9.10.5.2: explicit-Cx cancellation was being ignored/misclassified at the runtime_compat semaphore + generic Pool boundary, which let vendored mux-pool explicit-Cx paths surface timeout semantics or proceed instead of failing as cancellation. Landed fix in runtime_compat.rs, pool.rs, vendored/mux_pool.rs, and a small downstream guard in wezterm.rs so PoolError::Cancelled does not trip the vendored circuit breaker. Added focused regressions for pre-cancelled pool/mux-pool operations and mid-wait permit cancellation, plus proptest coverage for the new PoolError variant. Local rustfmt --check and git diff --check are green. Remote evidence is in progress via valid offloaded runs 58601 and 77601; a separate run 66978 failed the remote dependency preflight with RCH-E324 and fell open local, so it was rejected and not used as validation evidence.","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:24:36Z"},{"id":2612,"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.2","author":"SunnyRaven","text":"Support slice ft-e34d9.10.5.2.2 completed on the clean integration harness only: crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mux_migration_completion.rs. Added pool_cancelled_cx_under_contention_then_recovery_restores_capacity, which proves a cancelled nested acquire while the only pool permit is held surfaces cancellation, preserves zero available permits, does not increment total_acquired, does not issue an extra mock mux request, and still allows the next healthy release/retry path to restore balance. Remote validation passed via rch on worker vmi1153651 with env TMPDIR=/tmp CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target/rch-ft-e34d9-10-5-2-2 cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test mux_migration_completion --features asupersync-runtime,vendored pool_cancelled_cx_under_contention_then_recovery_restores_capacity -- --test-threads=1 => 1 passed / 0 failed.  {"id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.2.2","title":"[ft-e34d9.10.5.2][support] Strengthen cancellation-under-contention invariants in mux migration completion harness","description":"Bounded test-only support slice under ft-e34d9.10.5.2. Scope is limited to crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mux_migration_completion.rs. Strengthen the currently shallow cancellation coverage by adding a contention-focused invariant: a cancelled acquire while the only pool permit is held must fail cleanly without leaking capacity or causing spurious downstream mux activity, and the subsequent healthy release/retry path must restore a balanced pool. Validate with rch-only targeted cargo evidence.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SunnyRaven","created_at":"2026-03-30T00:11:39.606026Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-30T00:31:08.441229Z","closed_at":"2026-03-30T00:31:08.441066Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","cancellation","integration-tests","test-fix","vendored"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.2.2","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-30T00:11:39.606026Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2605,"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.2.2","author":"SunnyRaven","text":"Started bounded support slice on crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mux_migration_completion.rs only. Plan: strengthen cancellation-under-contention coverage so a cancelled nested acquire while the only pool permit is held fails cleanly without leaking capacity or causing spurious mux activity, then prove the subsequent healthy release/retry path restores balance. File reservation is active via Agent Mail #6191; validation will be rch-only targeted cargo.","created_at":"2026-03-30T00:11:57Z"},{"id":2607,"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.2.2","author":"SunnyRaven","text":"Patch landed in crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mux_migration_completion.rs only. Added a new cancellation-under-contention regression that mirrors the existing timeout-cascade recovery shape but uses user cancellation and stronger invariants: blocked acquire must surface cancellation, preserve zero available permits while the primary checkout is held, avoid incrementing total_acquired, and avoid issuing extra mock mux requests before a healthy release/retry restores balance. Local rustfmt and git diff --check are green. Targeted remote validation is in flight via rch on worker vmi1153651: env TMPDIR=/tmp CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target/rch-ft-e34d9-10-5-2-2 cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test mux_migration_completion --features asupersync-runtime,vendored pool_cancelled_cx_under_contention_then_recovery_restores_capacity -- --test-threads=1","created_at":"2026-03-30T00:17:11Z"},{"id":2611,"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.2.2","author":"SunnyRaven","text":"Remote validation completed cleanly on worker vmi1153651. Command: env TMPDIR=/tmp CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target/rch-ft-e34d9-10-5-2-2 cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test mux_migration_completion --features asupersync-runtime,vendored pool_cancelled_cx_under_contention_then_recovery_restores_capacity -- --test-threads=1. Result: 1 passed / 0 failed. This confirms the new regression in crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mux_migration_completion.rs correctly proves a cancelled nested acquire under contention does not leak permits, does not count as a successful checkout, does not issue an extra mux request, and still allows the following healthy release/retry path to restore a balanced pool.","created_at":"2026-03-30T00:30:29Z"}]}
-{"id":"ft-e8brh","title":"[MEDIUM] cancel_races_child_creation test has weak race coverage; passes trivially via propagation","description":"In commit 6a16ae0a (fix(ft-2xnop): add unix umask coverage ... +
cancellation concurrency tests), the test
\`cancel_races_child_creation_all_children_see_cancellation\` at
crates/frankenterm-core/src/cancellation.rs:2635-2676 claims to verify
the cancel+child race but does not reliably exercise the race window.

Structure:
\`\`\`rust
for _ in 0..20 {
    let parent = CancellationToken::new(...);
    let n_creators = 8;
    let barrier = Arc::new(Barrier::new(n_creators + 1));

    let handles: Vec<_> = (0..n_creators).map(|i| {
        let parent = parent.clone();
        let barrier = Arc::clone(&barrier);
        thread::spawn(move || {
            barrier.wait();
            parent.child(ScopeId(format!(\"c_{i}\")))
        })
    }).collect();

    barrier.wait();
    parent.cancel(ShutdownReason::UserRequested);
    // ... assert every child is cancelled
}
\`\`\`

Problems:

1. The barrier synchronizes the START of each thread's work but not
   the duration of \`parent.child(...)\`. On a machine with >=8 cores,
   the 8 creator threads typically finish \`parent.child(...)\` BEFORE
   the main thread reaches \`parent.cancel(...)\`. In that ordering, all
   children are pre-existing when cancel propagates — a trivial case
   covered by other tests in the file.

2. 20 sequential rounds with no scheduler perturbation (no \`yield_now\`,
   no sleep, no thread count above core count) rarely hit the interesting
   interleaving where cancel runs mid-child-creation.

3. The assertion passes even if \`parent.child()\` has a bug where it
   reads the \"parent cancelled\" flag AFTER registration but BEFORE
   propagation reaches it — because the test never creates that window.

Impact: false confidence in race-safe \`CancellationToken::child()\`
under concurrent cancel. A latent bug where a newly-created child
escapes both propagation (not-yet-registered) AND post-cancel detection
(child() reads stale flag) would not be caught.

Fix direction:
- Spawn more creators than CPU cores (e.g., 64) so some are preempted
  mid-call.
- Insert \`thread::yield_now()\` or a short sleep inside the creator
  body before calling \`parent.child()\` to widen the race window.
- Alternatively, use an inner barrier that creators wait on AFTER
  they've decided to create the child but BEFORE registration, so the
  main thread can force the interleaving.
- Consider using loom or shuttle for deterministic interleaving
  coverage instead of probabilistic races.

Severity: medium — test quality issue, not a proven correctness bug
in cancellation.rs. Filing because the test claims coverage it does
not deliver.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T07:25:11.821489Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T07:25:11.821489Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cancellation","concurrency","review","test-gap","testing"]}

cancellation concurrency tests), the test
\`cancel_races_child_creation_all_children_see_cancellation\` at
crates/frankenterm-core/src/cancellation.rs:2635-2676 claims to verify
the cancel+child race but does not reliably exercise the race window.

Structure:
\`\`\`rust
for _ in 0..20 {
    let parent = CancellationToken::new(...);
    let n_creators = 8;
    let barrier = Arc::new(Barrier::new(n_creators + 1));

    let handles: Vec<_> = (0..n_creators).map(|i| {
        let parent = parent.clone();
        let barrier = Arc::clone(&barrier);
        thread::spawn(move || {
            barrier.wait();
            parent.child(ScopeId(format!(\"c_{i}\")))
        })
    }).collect();

    barrier.wait();
    parent.cancel(ShutdownReason::UserRequested);
    // ... assert every child is cancelled
}
\`\`\`

Problems:

1. The barrier synchronizes the START of each thread's work but not
   the duration of \`parent.child(...)\`. On a machine with >=8 cores,
   the 8 creator threads typically finish \`parent.child(...)\` BEFORE
   the main thread reaches \`parent.cancel(...)\`. In that ordering, all
   children are pre-existing when cancel propagates — a trivial case
   covered by other tests in the file.

2. 20 sequential rounds with no scheduler perturbation (no \`yield_now\`,
   no sleep, no thread count above core count) rarely hit the interesting
   interleaving where cancel runs mid-child-creation.

3. The assertion passes even if \`parent.child()\` has a bug where it
   reads the \"parent cancelled\" flag AFTER registration but BEFORE
   propagation reaches it — because the test never creates that window.

Impact: false confidence in race-safe \`CancellationToken::child()\`
under concurrent cancel. A latent bug where a newly-created child
escapes both propagation (not-yet-registered) AND post-cancel detection
(child() reads stale flag) would not be caught.

Fix direction:
- Spawn more creators than CPU cores (e.g., 64) so some are preempted
  mid-call.
- Insert \`thread::yield_now()\` or a short sleep inside the creator
  body before calling \`parent.child()\` to widen the race window.
- Alternatively, use an inner barrier that creators wait on AFTER
  they've decided to create the child but BEFORE registration, so the
  main thread can force the interleaving.
- Consider using loom or shuttle for deterministic interleaving
  coverage instead of probabilistic races.

Severity: medium — test quality issue, not a proven correctness bug
in cancellation.rs. Filing because the test claims coverage it does
not deliver.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T07:25:11.821489Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T07:48:54.626483Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T07:48:54.626237Z","close_reason":"Hardened in 7ac677a6: creator count 8→64 (forces preemption on any host), per-thread yield_now + modulo 50µs sleep widens race, rounds 20→64, each creator samples is_cancelled() pre-child() and the round-level outcomes are checked so the test asserts the cancel-first ordering was actually exercised at least once — if it isn't, the test fails with a diagnostic pointing at loom/shuttle for deterministic coverage. Build clean; local run blocked by unrelated macOS dyld bottleneck under load.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cancellation","concurrency","review","test-gap","testing"]}
 {"id":"ft-gofz5","title":"[EPIC] Release engineering: first tagged release, cargo install validation, and binary distribution pipeline","description":"# Goal
Establish a working release pipeline so that \`cargo install --git ... ft\` actually works, release binaries are available for major platforms, and there are tagged versions users can reference.

# Background
The reality check found:
- Only 1 git tag exists ("backup-before-rewrite") — no version releases
- \`cargo install\` is advertised in README Quick Install but never validated
- 6 GitHub Actions workflows exist (ci.yml, release.yml, etc.) but release.yml has never been triggered for a real release
- The project is pre-1.0 with 4,288 commits but zero releases
- Build is resource-intensive (~793k LOC core library) which may cause cargo install to fail on memory-constrained machines

# Why This Matters
A project that can't be installed by its advertised method has zero users. Even for internal/swarm use, tagged releases let agents pin to known-good versions. Release hygiene signals project maturity.

# Architectural Intent
1. Validate that \`cargo install --git ... ft\` succeeds on a clean machine (Linux x86_64, macOS arm64 at minimum).
2. Create v0.1.0 tag with honest release notes reflecting current capabilities.
3. Ensure release.yml workflow produces downloadable binaries.
4. Add a CI job that runs \`cargo install\` from git to catch install regressions.

# Acceptance Criteria
1. \`cargo install --git https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/frankenterm.git ft\` succeeds on Linux x86_64 and macOS arm64.
2. A v0.1.0 tag exists with release notes that honestly describe working features, partial features, and known limitations.
3. release.yml produces downloadable binaries for at least Linux x86_64 and macOS arm64.
4. CI includes a cargo install smoke test that runs on every push to main.
5. README Quick Install section includes minimum system requirements (RAM, disk, Rust nightly version).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:55:35.783442Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-11T02:07:18.344985Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T02:07:18.344593Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","distribution","goal-line","release"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-gofz5","depends_on_id":"ft-d0ez0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:59:00.127254Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-gofz5.1","title":"Validate cargo install from git on clean Linux x86_64 and macOS arm64 environments","description":"# Goal
Prove that the advertised Quick Install command actually works on fresh machines.

# Design
1. On a clean Linux x86_64 environment (CI runner or fresh container):
   - Install Rust nightly (matching rust-toolchain.toml)
   - Run: cargo install --git https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/frankenterm.git ft
   - Verify: ft --version produces output
   - Verify: ft doctor runs without crash
   - Record: peak RAM usage, disk usage, wall time

2. On macOS arm64 (CI runner or local):
   - Same steps as above
   - Additional: verify LLVM/clang dependency resolution (cc alias issue documented in MEMORY.md)

3. If install fails, diagnose and fix:
   - Likely issues: memory exhaustion (793k LOC), missing system deps, cc alias conflict
   - Fix in Cargo.toml, build.rs, or CI configuration
   - Document minimum requirements

# Acceptance Criteria
1. cargo install succeeds on both platforms with exit code 0.
2. ft --version and ft doctor both work post-install.
3. Peak RAM and disk requirements documented.
4.    - Partial: tx execution (orchestration framework complete, data plane in progress), mission dispatch
   - Known limitations: resource-intensive build, Rust nightly required, WezTerm backend dependency
3. Trigger release.yml workflow
4. Verify downloadable binaries are produced for Linux x86_64 and macOS arm64

# Acceptance Criteria
1. Git tag v0.1.0 exists with annotated release notes.
2. GitHub Release page has downloadable binaries.
3. Release notes are honest — no feature is listed as working unless it passes end-to-end.
4.
